channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: Video: Overwatch Official Side-By-Side Comparison (Switch 1 & 2)
item.content:encoded:
Spot the difference

Blizzard's free-to-play team-based shooter Overwatch (previously known as Overwatch 2) made its debut on the Switch 2 last week, and while it didn't exactly get off to a smooth start, Blizzard was quick to respond with an update.

Now that the title is up and running on the Switch 2, Blizzard has shared a brief side-by-side comparison video, showing off the old Switch version of the game next to the new release. As noted, players can now experience "better visuals, higher fidelity audio, and up to 60 fps in both docked and handheld mode".

It doesn't go into any specifics beyond this, but as you can see in the video above, the environments now look a little sharper. Good Vibes Gaming investigated the Switch 2 version before the patch was released and found there was a slight improvement to resolution and textures compared to the Switch release.

Once again, if you want to jump into Overwatch yourself, it's free-to-play and can be downloaded right now from the eShop. And you don't have to worry about frame rate limits, as these issues have now been resolved in the latest game update.

Have you given Overwatch 2 a go on the Switch 2 yet? How are you finding it compared to the experience on Switch and other platforms? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/04/video-overwatch-official-side-by-side-comparison-switch-1-and-2
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Warhammer 40,000 Dawn of War IV Reveals the Adeptus Mechanicus, Tech Priests Relentlessly Pursuing Knowledge
item.content:encoded:
Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War IV has unveiled a new cinematic trailer for another of its four factions. This time, it focuses on the Adeptus Mechanicus, the tech priests’ actions, ancient dangers, and the potential for wide-ranging consequences from their relentless quest for knowledge.

The new trailer is the second (following the Orks) of the four scheduled narrative faction trailers for Deep Silver and KING Art Games’ upcoming sci-fi RTS. 

Devoted to the Omnissiah, the Adeptus Mechanicus pursue knowledge, and ancient technology. Then, they pursue even more of it. It is a relentless cycle for them, and in the cinematic, we see Magos Dominus Nulpherus-1 and a second tech priest both descending into a grim Necron tomb that is quiet and allegedly dormant. 

Of course, you wouldn't believe that for a second right? Maybe if you never played anything related to Warhammer before.

You can watch the age-restricted trailer directly on YouTube here.

Things begin to quickly become a lot less quiet and clearly not dormant. The situation changes and they are forced into a fight for survival as they also try to make their escape out of the depths of this tomb. These attempts at access to greater knowledge are part of the decisions you’ll have to make in the game, because that unquenchable thirst for knowledge (blended with religion and a believed sanctity of machines), just might lead them into some sticky situations.

Pushing forth with reckless ambition to acquire forbidden knowledge. Lead to big trouble? It’s a distinct possibility. 

You can watch the trailer in order to find out what happens, but there is an unexpectedly helpful arrival.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/warhammer-40000-dawn-of-war-iv-reveals-the-adeptus-mechanicus-tech-priests-relentlessly-pursuing-knowledge-2000137858
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Lenovo Legion Go S is RAMageddon’s latest victim
item.content:encoded:
Skip to main content

The best version nearly doubled in price overnight.

The best version nearly doubled in price overnight.

by

Apr 20, 2026, 10:49 PM UTC

Photo: Sean Hollister / The Verge

Sean Hollister

is a senior editor and founding member of The Verge who covers gadgets, games, and toys. He spent 15 years editing the likes of CNET, Gizmodo, and Engadget.

You can still find the Asus Xbox Ally X and the MSI Claw 8 AI Plus for $999 and $1,049 respectively, but Lenovo’s Legion Go S has seemingly given up the fight. The best version of Lenovo’s 8-inch handheld now costs nearly double what it did at launch — originally $829.99 last summer, the SteamOS version with Z1 Extreme chip now costs a staggering $1,579.99 at Best Buy.

That’s an even bigger price hike than with Lenovo’s flagship Legion Go 2, which saw up to a $650 price hike early this month.

Not every Legion Go S model costs nearly double what it did before, but none are anywhere near what they originally cost. The Windows Z1 Extreme model is listed at $1,679.99 now, though it’s “on sale” for $1,049.99. (B&H has it for $1,299.99.)

Even the Z2 Go model, which is quite a bit weaker, has seen its price go from $599.99 at launch to nearly $1,000 at Best Buy, $1,049 at Lenovo, and $1,199 at Walmart (for the Windows model). And while Amazon still seems to have a SteamOS unit for $714, I wouldn’t personally pay even that much for a Z2 Go.

Lenovo didn’t immediately respond to a request for comment, but I suspect the company is quietly axing this version in the face of RAMageddon. Many listings for various versions of the handheld have disappeared entirely from retailers and from Lenovo’s own website.

Perhaps Lenovo has a newer version coming, though? The company’s website is already beginning to refer to it as the “Lenovo Legion Go S Gen 1,” according to Google:

The global RAM shortage has laid waste to all kinds of hardware, but gaming gadgets have been especially hard hit. One does not simply buy a Steam Deck anymore, Meta hiked the price of the Quest 3 by $100 last week, the PlayStation 5 got a price hike, and the list goes on. You can follow all the hikes in this Verge StoryStream, and read our explainers about why it’s happening and how long it might take to resolve.

Asus rep Anthony Spence told me earlier this month that there’s “no price increase on the horizon, so far as I can tell,” for the Xbox Ally X in the US. He says that’s still true as of today.

Follow topics and authors from this story to see more like this in your personalized homepage feed and to receive email updates.

  • Sean Hollister

The Verge Daily

A free daily digest of the news that matters most.



https://www.theverge.com/games/915278/lenovo-legion-go-s-price-hike-discontinued-ramageddon
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Holy Moly, Crusader Kings 3 Will Let You Play as a Pope
item.content:encoded:
Crusader Kings 3 is getting a new core expansion, By God Alone, and it's going to add playable popes to the Paradox strategy game alongside other new gameplay mechanics to let players influence the church.

"For the first time in the Crusader Kings franchise, we're also introducing playable theocracies, allowing you to rise to the power of the Pope himself," said Mikael Andersson, lead designer on the expansion. "By God Alone extends your options of how to engage with your Christian faith and the church behind it. It gives you more strategic options for how to deal with this institution, as well as dealing with other rulers of the same faith as you."

By God Alone will arrive in Q3 of this year as a paid expansion, so you've plenty of time to figure out how to wield the word of god to your strategic advantage.

"In this core expansion, we're fleshing out the Christian church to make the institution itself feel like a living entity," Andersson said during a video reveal for the expansion. "We're revising the way the church collects taxes to make it more obvious how taxation from temple holdings are being collected and allow the player to direct it to some extent. During the time span of Crusader Kings 3, the Christian faith went through a lot of upheaval. To represent this, we're introducing rites, local variations of practice that the player can interact with."

The reveal video also talked about another core expansion, Silk & Silver, which will allow players to live that Middle Ages merchant life, establishing trade routes and undercutting their business rivals. There's also a free update dropping today, which adds "age-related ailments" for older characters and a new system for personal character-bound Stories within the game.

Crusader Kings 3 is a strategy game where you play through whole dynasties with plenty of drama, marriages, and feuds. Both expansions will add yet more depth to an already rich set of mechanics in the game that IGN's review called "a new king of historical strategy" when it was released in 2022.

Rachel Weber is the Head of Editorial Development at IGN and an elder millennial. She's been a professional nerd since 2006 when she got her start on Official PlayStation Magazine in the UK, and has since worked for GamesIndustry.Biz, Rolling Stone and GamesRadar. She loves horror, horror movies, horror games, Red Dead Redemption 2, and her Love and Deepspace boyfriends.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/holy-moly-crusader-kings-3-will-let-you-play-as-a-pope
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Reveal Footage Leaks Online
item.content:encoded:
Right after the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced reveal showcase announcement, footage and screenshots from the trailer have leaked.

Just a few hours ago, Ubisoft revealed that the worst-kept secret in gaming, the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Remake, will finally be revealed later this week. Now, it is doubling down on its reputation as the worst-kept secret, with details from the upcoming reveal event already surfacing online.

A user named @intercelluar on X (formerly Twitter) shared a short clip and several screenshots from the reveal trailer of Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, as spotted on r/GamingLeaksAndRumours. The footage lasts 11 seconds and highlights various scenes leading up to a naval battle. This includes moments such as spotting an enemy ship, the crew operating the cannons, Edward steering the ship, and cannonballs being hurled between the opposing ships.

The footage and screenshots also reveal the high fidelity the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Remake has achieved, with highly detailed character models, realistic textures, flashy explosions, and more showcased in the leaked trailer snippet. However, the leaked footage only shows cutscene events; we have yet to see any actual gameplay. The leaker also posted a screenshot that appears to show the reveal trailer is 1:08 seconds long. Interested readers can check out the leaked clip here.

In other news, Insider Gaming exclusively confirmed earlier this week that the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag remake will launch on July 9, 2026. Additionally, the Indonesian rating for the game confirms that players will receive new content. What are your thoughts on Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced’s reveal footage leaking ahead of the showcase? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about PlayStation’s requirement of age verification for certain online features. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/assassins-creed-black-flag-resynced-reveal-footage-leaks-online/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: New World: Aeternum Will Loosen More Restrictions, Turn on XP Bonus to Let Remaining Players Access Content
item.content:encoded:
New World: Aeternum is making more moves to loosen and lift caps and otherwise make accessing the game’s content simpler. This follows previous updates that began to make certain things easier to access (like unlocking all transmogs) and adjusted some requirements to account for a smaller population as the game remains in maintenance mode in its final nine months or so.

Tomorrow, at 9:00 a.m. Pacific time, there will be downtime for this next update. After about 90 minutes of down time, the following changes will be in effect:

The cap on Umbral Crystals will go up to 100k a week. Daily Gypsum acquisition limits will be boosted by 10x the current rate. This means Amethyst will go from 10 to 100, and Ruby will go from 3 to 30, and so on. You can check the announcement for all of the new numbers.

??Upcoming MaintenanceDowntime will begin on 4/21 at 9AM PT (4PM UTC), and last approximately 1.5 hours.??Release Notes• Umbral Crystals weekly limit will be increased to 100K• Daily Gypsum acquisition limit will be increased by 10x the current rate? Ruby: 30?…

— New World Game Status (@NWGameStatus) April 20, 2026

The limit on gold in inventory will increase to 10 million. Amazon is also  turning on a permanent Double XP bonus for characters, weapons, gathering, and refining. The team is also lifting the cap on house ownership, so if your character is over level 55 you can buy additional houses, up to five.

They are also removing the MSQ requirement for entering Elysian Wilds. So go, um, wild.

These changes make sense, given the situation, and without turning it into a complete free for all just yet. Though, towards the very end, that wouldn’t be such a bad idea.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/new-world-aeternum-will-loosen-more-restrictions-turn-on-xp-bonus-to-let-remaining-players-access-content-2000137857
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: New Minions Movie Retconning The Lore That Kept The Bastards Out Of WW1 And WW2
item.content:encoded:
Did you know that in the first Minions spin-off film released back in 2015, the movie explained why the loud and obnoxious characters who love to help evil people didn’t lend a hand to any leaders during World War I or World War 2? It’s true. But the latest entry in the franchise, Minions & Monsters, is apparently retconning all of this, and now I have questions.

This isn’t breaking news. Instead, it’s sort of been known for a bit now and was fully confirmed by Universal and Illumination during last week’s CinemaCon. But I missed that, so when I saw a social media post claiming that the new Minions movie showed off footage set during the 1920s and featuring the Minions becoming Classic Hollywood stars, my reaction was: Excuse me, what?

You can see some of this Hollywood action in this previously released trailer.

As I mentioned, the first Minions movie spends its opening minutes explaining the origin of the Minions and their role in history. They always helped out the baddest, most despicable people around, right up to Napoleon Bonaparte in 1812. While helping the French general, they screwed up badly and angered him. So they fled to the snowy north of Russia to hide out and build their own mini-civilization.

And they all stayed there until 1968, conveniently not leaving the cave until well after two of the biggest, bloodiest, and most horrific wars in human history had ended. Fans have long joked about how the creators made sure to canonically hide away the Minions for more than a century to sidestep the Hitler question. Because, yeah, if these Minions truly did try to help the worst, baddest villains around and they were hanging out in the 1930s, well…that would be awkward.

And yet, Minions & Monsters is retconning the ice cave and telling us that at least some of the Minions ventured out well before the 1960s. Now, it is possible that Minions & Monsters will have a solution for this problem. Perhaps the movie will end with the Minions returning to their ice cave after a fun adventure with a baby monster, and they stay there for another 40 years or so. That would make this less a retcon and more a tiny tweak to the timeline. I think the funnier way to solve this would be to have some background dialogue or a newspaper imply that, in the films’ universe, WW1 didn’t happen. It wouldn’t be the first time the Minions franchise has had a massive lore retcon.

Anyway, this was supposed to be a 250-word blog, and now I’m closing in on 450, so I’m going to stop thinking about this and move on. Minions & Monsters arrives in theaters on July 1.



https://kotaku.com/new-minions-movie-retconning-the-lore-that-kept-the-bastards-out-of-ww1-and-ww2-2000689159
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Super Mario Bros. Movie Storyboard Suggests Film Almost Had Cameos From Wart, Mouser, And…Rawk Hawk?
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve seen either or both of the recent Mario movies, you know that the films are basically an hour and a half each of rapid-fire references and cameos. Basically every major plot point, joke, action, and crowd scene in each film is another opportunity for the filmmakers to go, “Hey, remember this guy/thing?!” Given that, it perhaps will shock you none at all to learn that even more cameos may have been planned for the The Super Mario Bros. Movie, including what would have been an utterly ridiculous pull from Paper Mario: The Thousand-Year Door, of all things.

This comes from the professional website of Douglas Lovelace, a storyboard animator with a robust resume spanning Lucasfilm, DreamWorks Animation, and more. Lovelace was apparently an uncredited storyboard artist on The Super Mario Bros. Movie, and in his portfolio he’s shared what seem to be a number of storyboard sketches from his work on the film. Specifically, it looks like Lovelace worked on the big wedding scene between Peach and Bowser, and from the looks of his art, a number of cameos were originally planned for that scene that didn’t make it into the final version.

In the storyboards you can see characters like Wart, Birdo, and Mouser sitting in the crowd for the wedding, none of whom wound up appearing in the The Super Mario Bros. Movie, instead eventually showing up in The Super Mario Galaxy Movie; Wart’s even a prominent villain! The storyboards show Birdo in particular as the subject of an awkward gag in which, when Toad objects to the marriage, she plops an egg out of her…nozzle.

But the most interesting cameo in here is this weird yellow bird guy sitting coolly, watching Peach walk down the aisle. Recognize him? That’s Rawk Hawk from Paper Mario: The Thousand-Year Door! If you’re unfamiliar, Rawk Hawk is the top fighter in the Glitz Pit, a wrestling arena where Mario has to fight his way to the top to earn a Champion Belt, and the Crystal Star attached to it. Rawk Hawk is a jerk of a character, constantly bragging about himself and degrading everyone else, though he gains some level of respect for Mario—sorry, “The Great Gonzales” after Gonzales thrashes him in the ring.

Rawk Hawk being present in The Super Mario Bros. Movie would have absolutely been one of those wacky, super deep, blink-and-you-miss-it cuts that most people would have missed or misunderstood entirely and read about online later. What amuses me the most about this particular storyboard is that here, he almost looks a bit like Fox McCloud (or, Falco, I guess), complete with arrogant expression and sick boots. Fox, of course, shows up in The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, and Rawk Hawk is nowhere to be found in either.

While I lament that both Mario movies are basically long reference-fests ala Ready Player One, I can’t deny that there’s a piece of my brain pleasantly tickled when I see one of Nintendo’s weirdest, most obscure dudes get all of one second in the spotlight. Though none of these guys made it into the wedding scene, King Boo did! So did King Bob-omb! It’s pleasantly silly to me that fellow royalty showed up to the marriage of the millennium. Honestly, forget the big cameos like Fox and the Babies Mario and Luigi. I’ll be rooting for Goombella in the next one (also if we don’t get Wario and Waluigi soon I am going to riot).

Check out the rest of the storyboard, too. The fight scene is really cool.



https://kotaku.com/super-mario-bros-movie-cameos-wart-jr-mouser-birdo-rawk-hawk-2000689128
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pokémon TCG Has Two New Ascended Heroes Releases Dropping This Month
item.content:encoded:
April 2026 is turning out to be a mega month in the Pokémon Trading Card Game calendar. Players will be able to get their hands on two new releases from the Mega Evolution—Ascended Heroes expansion when they drop on April 24, 2026. Whether you prefer volume pack opening, or love collecting rare promos, there’s something for everyone with these two new products.

The first release is a brand new Ascended Heroes Booster Bundle. If you’ve been struggling to get your hands on Pokémon’s recent Trading Card Game set, this could be your lucky day!

Pokémon TCG: Mega Evolution - Ascended Heroes Booster Bundle

Each Booster Bundle comes with six booster packs, each including 10 cards, 1 Basic Energy, and 1 Pokémon TCG Live code card. That’s right - Trainers' Pokémon and Stellar Tera Pokémon ex join returning and newly discovered Mega Evolution Pokémon ex, including new illustration rare and special rare cards to collect.

If you want to pick up the Pokémon TCG: Mega Evolution—Ascended Heroes Booster Bundle on 24 April 2026, you'd best be quick. Ascended Heroes has been flying off the shelves. Your best bet is always the official Pokémon Center for MSRP - $23.94.

Preorders aren't currently live here, so keep checking back. You can also find pre-order listings at reputable third-party reseller TCGPlayer, but the cheapest is currently $102.95 + $29.99 shipping.

The second new Ascended Heroes product, later this month, is technically three products. You can get three new Mega ex Boxes, each focusing on Pokémon Legends: Z-A’s partner Pokémon. Who will you choose? Each collection features a stunning foil promo card starring one of three new Mega Evolutions of Pokémon Legends: Z-A’s Partner Pokémon - Mega Meganium ex, Mega Emboar ex, or Mega Feraligatr ex.

Ascended Heroes - Mega Meganium ex, Mega Emboar ex, and Mega Feraligatr ex Boxes

Visually, these cards are a treat for long-time collectors. The vibrant, jagged energy lines bouncing out of the frame remind me of the iconic M Pokémon-EX from the XY Series, or the more recent Mega Evolution Special Illustration Rares designed by Saboteri. Whether you’re currently exploring the sectors of Lumiose City in Pokémon Legends: Z-A or you’re finally grinding out the post-game Battle Royale to pick up Mega Floette for the jump to Pokémon Champions, these promos are gorgeous additions to any binder.

Beyond their looks, Mega Meganium ex, Mega Emboar ex, and Mega Feraligatr ex all pack a punch. Each boasts devastating attacks designed to work perfectly with existing cards from their respective evolution chains. Blossoming with new power, Mega Meganium ex presents your opponent’s Pokémon with a Giant Bouquet filled with massive damage. Surrounded by flames, Mega Emboar ex lets loose a Crimson Blast to devastate your opponent. And with gnashing teeth and claws, Mega Feraligatr ex targets your opponent’s weakened Pokémon with a Mortal Crunch.

You’ll also get one oversize lenticular promo card featuring Mega Meganium ex, Mega Emboar ex, or Mega Feraligatr ex. While fan opinion on these oversized cards is split, seeing as you’d need the biggest binder known to man to fit one, it’s still a nice to have. The real prize is the four Pokémon TCG: Mega Evolution—Ascended Heroes booster packs inside.

While you can wait for pre-orders to go live at the Pokemon Center, Amazon has just restocked pre-orders for these collections at $36.99. It’s worth noting you can’t pick your Partner Pokémon here - you’ll receive, at random, one Pokémon TCG: Mega Evolution—Ascended Heroes ex box featuring either Mega Meganium ex, Mega Emboar ex, or Mega Feraligatr ex. Individual selection is not available.

While smaller additions to the Pokémon Trading Card Game 2026 release calendar, these two launches are set to curb the out of control hype for Ascended Heroes. Will you be picking up one of these expansions?

Sara Heritage is a journalist and freelance contributor, with 8 years of experience in gaming journalism. When she's not gaming, you’ll probably find her building yet another LEGO set I absolutely did not need (yes, I’ve got thoughts on the Pokémon LEGO).



https://www.ign.com/articles/pokemon-tcg-ascended-heroes-new-releases-april-2026
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The CyberPowerPC RTX 5070 Gaming PC With 32GB of DDR5 RAM Drops to $1399, Includes Pragmata
item.content:encoded:
The CyberPowerPC RTX 5070 Gaming PC With 32GB of DDR5 RAM Drops to $1399, Includes Pragmata

One of the least expensive RTX 5070 prebuilts I've seen this year, and now includes a hot new PC game.

Despite the rising prices of graphics cards and DDR5 RAM, there are still good deals to be found on prebuilt gaming PCs. B&H Photo is offering one such excellent deal this week. The CyberPowerPC Gamer Supreme Liquid gaming PC, featuring a GeForce RTX 5070 GPU, is on sale for just $1399 with free shipping after a $300 off instant discount. This is a well-equipped prebuilt that pairs the GPU with an AMD Ryzen 7 processor and a generous amount of RAM and storage.

Save $300 Off the CyperPowerPC RTX 5070 Gaming PC

CyberPowerPC Gamer Supreme AMD Ryzen 7 8700F GeForce RTX 5070 Gaming PC (32GB/2TB)

The CyberPowerPC Liquid Gamer Supreme gaming PC is equipped with a liquid-cooled AMD Ryzen 7 8700F CPU, GeForce RTX 5070 12GB GPU, 32GB of DDR5-6000MHz RAM, and a 2TB M.2 SSD. The AMD Ryzen 7 8700F CPU is an 8-core 16-thread processor with a max boost clock of 5GHz. It's a solid general-purpose Zen 4 CPU that combines a high clock speed and plenty of cores for unhindered gaming performance. Zen 4 CPUs is also compatible with newer DDR5 memory.

The GeForce RTX 5070 GPU is an excellent choice for 1080p or 1440p gaming

The GeForce RTX 5070 GPU is an excellent choice for 1080p or 1440p gaming. Compared to the previous generation GPUs, the RTX 5070 offers a slight performance improvement over the RTX 4070 Super, which was and still is an excellent GPU. The fps gain is greater in games that support DLSS 4.5 with multi-frame generation. Check out our RTX 5070 review.

Pragmata makes full use of all of Nvidia's most recent tech, including DLSS 4.5 with multi-frame generating, path tracing, ray reconstruction, and super resolution. It will run at 60fps and beyond on an RTX 5070 at 1440p, even without DLSS.

Eric Song is the IGN commerce manager in charge of finding the best gaming and tech deals every day. When Eric isn't hunting for deals for other people at work, he's hunting for deals for himself during his free time.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/cyberpowerpc-rtx-5070-gaming-pc-deal-includes-pragmata
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Monsters & Memories Pushes Back Early Access Start to October, But There Will Be More Content
item.content:encoded:
Monsters & Memories has pushed back the start of Early Access to October 1st. The Niche Worlds Cult team explains why they made the decision as well as why what we see in October just may be better than what we would’ve seen in June.

It's unlikely to surprise anyone that the team is pushing back the start of Early Access to put in some additional work, a decision they are “not something we’ve taken lightly”, they explain. Back in December, the team released a list of work they intended to get done before Early Access, and while they’ve been working on those things, progress has been slower than they had hoped. 

Over the past year, the all volunteer team has also brought on 20 more people, with half of them joining this year and some roles being shuffled around. So in addition to game development they've also had to onboard a bunch of people and get everyone coordinated and on the track to get them to their Early Access goal.

As for some silver linings, the beta, including the recently-started PvP-only beta option, will continue until Early Access begins.The team is now planning two additional starting areas and races for Early Access launch. This brings the total to four, and the Deep Elves and Deep Gnomes will also be playable. Early Access will still be a time of ongoing development before a full launch.

There will be a new open playtest from May 29th through June 7th and the team is going to continue scheduling more playtests. The full blog goes into tons of detail about what they are focusing on over the next few months to make the new date, some beta stats, and some answers to potential questions about the whole move.

Recently, co-founder and development lead Nick departed the studio. At the time, the team's statement said "there was no impact on our posted target dates", but would update should that change, and now they have. It seems that the reasons for pushing the date back go beyond this matter, and may have little to do with it.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/monsters-and-memories-pushes-back-early-access-start-to-october-but-there-will-be-more-content-2000137854
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Windrose Celebrates 1 Million Copies Sold In Less Than One Week
item.content:encoded:
Early Access pirate survival game, Windrose, has sold through 1 million copies in just six days, according to developer Kraken Express. 

The survival game launched on Steam last week and has become an instant hit, as Kraken Express has announced it has sold over 1 million copies on Steam since last Tuesday. The pirate survival game sees players explore the archipelago of Caribbean-influenced islands, building their pirate base, smuggling supplies with various factions, and sailing the high seas, taking the fight to the infamous Blackbeard and his pirate raiders. 

Via today's announcement:

"Ahoy, captains! We have just plundered another milestone – 1,000,000 copies, and “we” means “you” – a crew of fine brave captains sailing the seas of Windrose. Moreover, today Windrose has assembled over 200,000 concurrent players! Your support for the game is absolutely astonishing, and your patience while we are working on fixes is truly heart-warming. While we keep working on the game, we read your reviews and comments, watch your videos and streams, and sometimes, honestly, shed a pirate-y tear of happiness, because now when the game is out you share our dream of swashbuckling adventure. And there are boars, too, we know, but whatever does not send you flying into the nearest tree, makes you stronger, right?

Thank you for this, captains. You are our inspiration in this journey, and YARRRRR, that’s going to be an exciting one."

It's proven to be a hit, and I've personally put entirely too much time into it over the last week as I've been working on our review. The mix of casual survival mechanics that remind me of Valheim while providing a pretty compelling pirate fantasy has clicked in a way that I didn't think would be possible from the start. 

The team isn't just celebrating the 1 million copy milestone, though, as the early access title also saw a peak concurrent player count on Steam of 222K players yesterday. This is despite the game struggling with connection issues on hosted servers that caused issues early on. 

Yesterday, the Kraken Express team released a hotfix to address this, with direct IP connections added to the connection browser, as well as fixes targeted at save backups, especially if you're playing on multiple devices (like I am). You can check out the details on the Steam page, and keep an eye out for our review-in-progress this week.  



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/windrose-celebrates-1-million-copies-sold-in-less-than-one-week-2000137856
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Take-Two Boss Strauss Zelnick Suggests AI Could Take Elon Musk's Job
item.content:encoded:
Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick has suggested that Elon Musk might want to watch out for artificial intelligence taking his job after the Tesla boss proposed that Grand Theft Auto 6 could be generated by AI.

The gaming bigwig, who oversees major subsidiaries within Take-Two like 2K and Rockstar Games, shared his insight into the rapid advancements in technology during a conversation at Semafor World Economy 2026. It was a quick conversation that touched on a number of topics, but it was the issue of AI that probably received the most attention.

After saying he feels "people spend too much time talking about the 'woe is me' risk" related to the tech despite the fact that it “can be used for evil purposes," Zelnick brought up Musk and his relationship to AI, unprompted. While Zelnick is optimistic about how AI can be used as a tool for the average worker, he isn’t so sure the billionaire X/Twitter owner’s job is quite as safe.

"If AI were going to get rid of employment, the richest man on Earth, Elon Musk, knows a little something about AI, last time I checked," the Take-Two head said. "He has unlimited financial resources, and he has unlimited human resources, and he has, apparently, an unlimited number of ideas. He knows his way around AI.

"The man works 20 hours a day. If AI were going to take anyone’s job, wouldn’t it take his job? The richest guy on Earth, wouldn’t that be job number one for AI to take? Why is he so busy? By the way, why am I working harder than ever despite the fact that I’ve totally accepted AI into every part of my life?"

Zelnick’s comments follow an exchange that happened on Musk’s social media platform earlier this year, where he said, "yeah," in response to a post that said, "there is definitely a non-zero chance we get ‘generate your own GTA 6 in a few minutes’ before GTA 6." Rockstar’s latest Grand Theft Auto game, which has been in development for around six years and is poised to be one of the biggest video game launches ever, is currently set with a release date of November 19, 2026.

You won’t even have to ask. AI will figure what video game you’d like best.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) January 14, 2026

Zelnick previously offered a more indirect response to Musk in March, saying at the time that the idea AI could generate quality video games "laughable." He added at the time, "These tools may help you create assets, but that won’t help you create hits."

Zelnick’s thoughts on Musk and his relationship with AI came to an end with a joke about how the Tesla billionaire could be "a simulation." As the audience laughed, he said, "In fairness, if you were going to choose a person who were a simulation, he would be my number one choice."

GTA 6 is currently expected to launch later this year. While we wait, you can read about the fake Grand Theft Auto leaks that started gaining traction on social media late last year.

Michael Cripe is a freelance writer with IGN. He's best known for his work at sites like The Pitch, The Escapist, and OnlySP. Be sure to give him a follow on Bluesky (@mikecripe.bsky.social) and Twitter (@MikeCripe).

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/take-two-boss-strauss-zelnick-suggests-ai-could-take-elon-musks-job
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced reveal showcase for April 23
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: After All These Years, it’s Moon Child
item.content:encoded:
You likely have not played the Dutch platformer Moon Child. The game was developed by Team Hoi and started its life on Amiga in 1993, only to eventually end up on Windows after a troubled development and a limited release. But thanks to an excellent write-up from the blog Games That Weren’t about the unfinished Amiga version, as well as a post featuring the main theme going viral on Bluesky, Moon Child has taken on a new life. This is due to the fact that the theme for the game goes crazy hard. People can’t get enough of Moon Child.

Moon Child’s unreleased Amiga theme is distinct from the final Windows theme, particularly when it comes to the lyrics. In particular, the lyric “you’ve got the power to be his friend” is a challenge and affirmation to the listener. The song, sung by composer Ramon Braumuller, was recorded on an ADAT multitrack and sampled by the Amiga. It is interspersed with samples and goes like this:

It’s Moon Child.
Wah-ah-wo-ho.
You’ve got the power to be his friend.
It’s Moon Child.
Wah-ah-wo-ho.
“It’s the beginning of a new and excitingly different story!”
(This last line is a sample originally from The Adventures of Superman radio series, probably via a sample CD. The Chrono Trigger arrange CD The Brink of Time also samples this.)

It’s Moon Child.
Wah-ah-wo-ho.
You’ve got the power to be his friend. (4x)
“The story…is true.”
(This sample is originally from Dragnet [1987], but the sample as edited appears in The Art Of Noise - Dragnet [Art Of Noise '88 12" Mix])

Moon Child is having its moment in the sun now, but what led to its development is a rocky history.

Difficult times

Moon Child was the product of Dutch software developers Team Hoi. Their first outing in 1992, Hoi, was also a platformer, starring a little green guy named Hoi. Team Hoi had become frustrated with how their first game had turned out, in part due to how the game was handled by multiple publishers, starting with Innerprise Software.

“When Hoi was about 60 percent finished, Innerprise Software asked us to send them the latest version of the game, for internal evaluation and testing purposes,” developer Metin Seven told me via email. “About three weeks later the Hoi version we sent to Innerprise turned out to have been leaked to the Amiga hacker Fairlight, and was rapidly being copied by Amiga users around the world.”

This led the team to cancel their agreement with Innerprise and move to a developer known as Hollyware. “But following the release of Hoi, Hollyware never paid us any royalties apart from a $200 cheque ‘for the release celebration party,’” Metin told me. Unable to fight back legally, they took their lumps, only for a deception similar to the Hollyware situation to happen to them again with the release of their game Clockwiser and UK publisher Rasputin Software. 

“Both Hoi and Clockwiser were received warmly by the international game press, which made the events with the publishers even more sour.”

Undeterred, Metin said, the team vowed to create a new platformer for the Amiga starring an elfin green creature with Hoi as the sidekick. By the time they had finished the game Commodore had gone bankrupt, making Amiga’s future uncertain and publishing on the platform a rocky prospect at best. Eventually they would transition the game entirely to Windows, ditching Hoi as a sidekick and focusing on Moon Child. 

Unfortunately for Team Hoi, the company they had made publishing arrangements with, Dutch multimedia company Valkieser, had made the disastrous choice to invest heavily in both hardware and software for the Phillips CD-i, one of the most infamous console bombs in gaming history. The lack of funds caused them to terminate their publishing wing and, as a result, the Windows version of Moon Child’s release was limited to the Netherlands. 

“Later, we found out that the game had lived a life of its own in the worldwide piracy circuit, and I still receive e-mails every now and then from people expressing the childhood joy Moon Child delivered, which is really heart-warming,” Metin told me.

A Second Life

Thanks to the aforementioned viral post about Moon Child, its unreleased theme has taken on a life of its own. The bumping track has its roots in the Commodore 64 and the demo scene. Composer Ramon originally created a music editor called SIDmon to create his earliest music. “Later, we developed a new, more capable music editor called Digital Mugician, which was capable of adding three CPU-powered audio channels to the Amiga's four audio chip channels.” Metin said “The Moon Child Amiga demo title track was made with that.”

“I started playing drums at age six and joined bands by 12. In the 90s, my brother and I produced house music in our parents' home studio under the name 'The R,'” Ramon told me via email. “I believe the blend of those house influences with the Amiga demo scene tracker music formed the foundation for the Moon Child track.” 

I became aware of Moon Child as fan art and memes began to drip into my feed on the 15th of April. Edits, often by user @ricesnot.bsky.social, began to flood the timeline. Fanart from various artists built up. What was once an obscure character in a vintage Dutch Windows game was now an object of adoration.

In response to this long overdue love, Metin uploaded all the source files and assets to the game onto the Internet Archive. “I almost can't express how joyful it is to witness all the love for Moon Child and our other games,” Metin told me “It's a wonderful, surreal, wild ride, and it pretty much makes up for the repeated bad luck we experienced during our game development years.” 

Though the team had moved on from game development after a rocky run, this sudden outpouring of love feels like a kind of karmic payback. Moon Child had always been with us, a green-eared little guy lurking in the background, waiting for decades for the people to realize that they had the power to be his friend. 



https://aftermath.site/moon-child-team-hoi-revival-memes/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: 15 Tips To Know Before Starting Windrose, That New Pirate Game On Steam Everyone Is Playing
item.content:encoded:
Windrose, out now on PC, is an open-world survival RPG all about being a pirate, having a big ship, and blasting away other boats. (You’ll also fight for loot and, of course, enjoy the classic pirate cliche of running away from wild hogs that will kill you in seconds.) Windrose, which is one of the biggest games on Steam right now, can be a hard and sometimes obtuse game, so here are 15 tips and tricks to help you as you begin your journey across the open seas, either alone or with some friends.

Always carry some grass, rocks, and wood

Living the pirate life in Windrose, especially early on, is less about swashbuckling adventures on the open seas and more about surviving dark, dangerous islands filled with feral wild hogs who will gut you in seconds. To help you survive, keep wood, rocks, and plant fibers with you at all times. You don’t need to fill your inventory, but a few bits of each can come in handy when landing on a strange new island. You’ll be able to quickly start fires, build torches, erect walls, and most importantly: make a tent.

Build tents when exploring new islands or before fights

Perhaps the most important tip to pull from this entire post is that tents are cheap and fast to build and let you respawn after death. The game explains this, sure, but keep in mind you can build tents basically anywhere in the open world. So before you rush headlong into a temple or enemy camp, pop a tent down and set it as your respawn point. That way, if you die, you spawn just a few feet away and not all the way back on the last island you explored or the beach where you landed earlier. This is very handy as combat can be a pain in the ass in Windrose. Speaking of…

Lower some sliders to have more fun

If you leave Windrose on its default normal difficulty settings and hop into a world, you’ll quickly discover that boars in this universe will mess you up. In fact, all enemies can kill you quickly. Now, you can try to mitigate this by upgrading your gear, being very careful during fights, and carrying a shit-ton of healing items on you at all times.

Or, you can just lower your game session’s sliders before starting. I lowered how much damage enemies give and their max health by about 25 percent. That change was enough to make the combat still tricky, but not quite as Dark Souls-y. Of course, if you prefer harder combat, keep the sliders where they are. But if you and your pals are tired of getting two-shotted by boars, even after leveling up, drop the sliders a bit and have a good time. I won’t tell anyone. And neither will all the people on the game’s subreddit who suggest the same thing.

Make sure to carry food with you

While out and about doing pirate stuff, you don’t want to be hungry. It’ll lower your max health. So make sure you carry some food with you at all times. Early on, crabs, bananas, and coconuts provide some useful recipes.

Parry attacks and lock on to enemies

Melee combat in Windrose is a lot easier if you lock onto enemies and parry attacks. Neither of these mechanics is very well explained, however. Parrying doesn’t use stamina and can stun enemies, offering a nice moment to attack. To parry, just tap block right as an attack is about to land. The timing is pretty generous, from what I can tell. On a controller, you can lock onto a target by clicking the right stick.

Use bonfires to heal

In Windrose, bonfires are what turn a random tent into a full-on campsite where you can even have your crew hang out. Even better, bonfires can heal you over time and help your stamina regenerate faster. So whenever you decide to call a spot home for an extended amount of time, pop down a bonfire.

Complete missions and clear points of interest to level up

You don’t level up by killing stuff in Windrose. Instead, you gain XP and level up by clearing points of interest around the map and completing quests. The points of interest are only considered clear once you’ve completed the objectives listed. This often requires finding a few chests hidden around the immediate area. Once you’ve cleared a location, it’ll be marked with a checkmark. Most islands have at least a few POIs to investigate, and they offer a fast way to gain some XP.

Increase your vitality stat ASAP

When you do level up, I recommend adding a few points to your max health by upgrading your vitality stat. Don’t focus exclusively on this stat, as the other attributes are useful too, but early on, having more health will help you fend off all those damn wild hogs.

You can fast-travel on your boat

After you get a boat and start exploring the map, you’ll encounter fast-travel points at certain towns and faction HQs. You can fast-travel to any of these places while commanding your boat or ship. Just open the map while at the wheel, select the fast-travel point, and a few seconds later you’ll be teleported to the spot.

Oh, and build more fast-travel spots

It’s a good idea to build a fast-travel bell at your main home base. You’ll get a free fast-travel bell early on in the game. After that, you’ll need to craft more of them using a workbench, copper, and rope. You can have up to 10 of them on the map at any given time, so use these to build out a transportation network to help fill in the gaps between towns and bases. Just keep in mind that fast-travel bells must be built by the ocean.

Build a wharf to upgrade and customize your vessel

Another thing that needs to be built by the water is a wharf. These let you customize, repair, and upgrade your ship or even build a whole new vessel, assuming you have the materials to do so.

Don’t board enemy ships right away

As you start to sail the high seas, you’ll quickly encounter enemy ships. These can be attacked and either sunk or boarded. Boarding grants you better rewards, but is riskier as you might have to face off against three or four enemy pirates. So don’t just hop right in. Instead, assuming you have the bullets and gunpowder, stay on your ship and pelt the enemies with gunfire. Weakening a few or even taking some out can help your crew and give you a better chance of successfully raiding the enemy ship.

When playing co-op, bring multiple ships

While riding on your buddy’s cool pirate ship and manning the guns is fun, you’re going to want to have multiple boats if possible. Having more than one ship helps you take on groups of enemies and means no one person is getting targeted by every cannon. Just make sure you split the loot fairly, of course.

When marking a spot on the map, select the off mini-map option

Here’s a tiny detail that I didn’t know about until I’d spent quite a bit of time with the game. When you mark a spot on the map, there’s a check box that appears at the bottom of the pop-up box. This asks if you want this mark to appear even if it’s not on the mini-map. Check that, and you’ll be able to mark faraway places and see the spot on the edge of your minimap, making navigation much easier.

To change sea shanties, stop and start your crew’s singing

Tired of the song your crew is singing? Just hold the button that starts them singing, which will stop them, and then hold it again to start them back up, and they’ll be singing a new song. Soon enough, you’ll get them singing “What do you do with a drunken sailor?! What do you do with a drunken sailor?!” or whatever song you so desire.



https://kotaku.com/15-tips-to-before-starting-windrose-that-new-pirate-game-on-steam-everyone-is-playing-2000689110
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: VGC GOAT Wolfey Glick Defends Pokémon Champions’ Limited Roster
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon VGC legend and content creator Wolfe ‘Wolfey’ Glick, aka WolfeyVGC, has publicly claimed that he is enjoying how simple Pokémon Champions is.

Wolfey stated on his Twitter: “I don’t agree that Champions needs an evolution (immediately), and I think the increased accessibility and lowered barrier to entry is well worth a limited time with fewer tools available to veterans of the game.”

Many VGC veterans have been frustrated with the small number of options the game has and have been questioning when Pokémon Champions will receive more eligible Pokémon.

Out of the 1025 unique Pokémon, Champions only has 187 playable options. Additionally, many commonly used items are missing, like Assault Vest, Choice Band, and Rocky Helmet.

Wolfey himself has competed in countless Pokémon tournaments; he was the first player to ever have won a Regional, National, International and World Championship.

Yet, Wolfey claims that the game doesn’t need to rush to add more content.

Wolfey Praises Pokémon Champions’ Simplicity

After IGN released an article claiming that Pokémon Champions needs to evolve, Wolfey argued:

“One of my favorite things about Pokemon as a competitive game is the sheer amount of options it gives the player in teambuilding, so it may be surprising that I actually think the limited options Champions presents is actually a huge win in many ways.”

The popular creator went on to explain that he’s glad that the game has focused on the new player experience, saying:

“Pokemon Champions has one very clear goal – to make competitive Pokemon, a deceptively complex game, accessible. Like many experienced players, I found the lack of items available at game launch to be jarring, but I realize now that with the broad goal in mind, it makes sense.”

“This more limited selection keeps the core unchanged of what makes competitive Pokemon fun, while simplifying it enough that it isn’t daunting for new players. It’s not obvious why a hidden item would stop Fake Out (covert cloak) or a Pokemon survive a strong hit (Assault Vest),” Wolfey added.

Pokémon Champions has removed the need to grind hours and hours in Pokémon games to perfectly set up a competitive team. That, along with a simpler meta, is allowing new players to learn the basics.

The limited options have also created an interesting challenge for some players who have to get more creative to answer the meta.

For example, some players are running Stalwart on Archuladon instead of the defence increases of Stamina because it allows them to hit the very powerful Mega Floette super effectively with a steel move while ignoring target-swapping moves like Follow Me.

More items, Pokémon, and battle gimmicks will undoubtedly be added to Pokémon Champions as time goes on, but starting with a slower, smaller set and scaling from there is a very smart way to approach the release of the free-to-play title.

Have you been enjoying Pokémon Champions? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And try new ways to tackle the meta with a clever trick which allows you more Pokémon Champions team slots without spending money.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/vgc-goat-wolfey-glick-defends-pokemon-champions-limited-roster/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook Steps Down, John Ternus To Take Over
item.content:encoded:
Apple is set to get a new CEO. On Monday afternoon, it was announced that CEO Tim Cook will be stepping down from his role later this year.

Cook will remain on as Apple CEO until September 1, 2026. As of that date, senior vice president of hardware engineering John Ternus will take over. Cook will then transition to being executive chairman on Apple’s board of directors.

“It has been the greatest privilege of my life to be the CEO of Apple and to have been trusted to lead such an extraordinary company,” Cook said in a statement.

Cook, 65, has been with Apple since 1998, and its CEO since August 2011. He took over for Steve Jobs after previously serving as chief operating officer. During his time as CEO, Apple has launched products like the AirPods and Apple Watch. Apple’s market value has also grown under Cook from $350 billion to $4 trillion.

Meet John Ternus, Apple’s New CEO

Starting September 1, Ternus will take over as Apple CEO. Ternus joined the company in 2001 as part of the product design team. He was promoted to vice president of hardware engineering in 2013, joining the executive team in 2021.

In the company announcement, Apple called Ternus “instrumental” in the design and launch of products like the iPad and AirPods, along with major updates to the iPhone, Mac, and Apple Watch.

“John Ternus has the mind of an engineer, the soul of an innovator, and the heart to lead with integrity and with honor,” Cook said of his successor. “He is a visionary whose contributions to Apple over 25 years are already too numerous to count, and he is without question the right person to lead Apple into the future. I could not be more confident in his abilities and his character, and I look forward to working closely with him on this transition and in my new role as executive chairman.”

Ternus says that he’s grateful for the opportunity to lead Apple after spending over two decades with the company.

“It has been a privilege to help shape the products and experiences that have changed so much of how we interact with the world and with one another,” he said. “I am filled with optimism about what we can achieve in the years to come, and I am so happy to know that the most talented people on earth are here at Apple, determined to be part of something bigger than any one of us.”

In other tech news, read about the massive settlement NZXT agreed to in response to a class action lawsuit over its Flex PC rental program. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/apple-ceo-tim-cook-steps-down-john-ternus-to-take-over/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Age verification will roll out for PlayStation in the UK and Ireland
item.content:encoded:
Sony has announced that age verification will be rolling out later this year for PlayStation users in both the UK and Ireland. Without age verification, PlayStation users will not be able to use any of the communication features, broadcasting features, and some in game features. You can see the announcement below.

“Age verification will be required later in 2026 for UK & Ireland adult accounts to access certain features. If you’re prompted, we recommend completing age verification to avoid interruptions later in 2026. We use age verification to deliver the best PlayStation experience for your age and to help protect our younger users.

“If you have not completed age verification, you can continue playing on PlayStation but communication features won’t be available until you do. To keep using communication, broadcasting, and certain in-game features without interruption, we recommend verifying your age now. Verify now or scan this QR code on your mobile device.”

The QR code mentioned above can be found here within the announcement and FAQ.

There are three different ways to verify your age to continue accessing the communication features. That includes a facial scan through third party company Yoti, using ID age verification with a passport, driving license, or national ID, or through mobile phone age verification. This last method checks your age that your mobile phone provider has on their records.

Source: PlayStation



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/20/age-verification-will-roll-out-for-playstation-in-the-uk-and-ireland-in-june/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Interview: Double Fine's Kiln Feels Surprisingly Like A MOBA
item.content:encoded:
Double Fine Productions has always been a studio creating odd games. It’s best known for Psychonauts, an excellent platformer that’s surprisingly one of the studio’s more straightforward games. Since then, Double Fine has created some weirdly charming adventures like Costume Quest, Massive Chalice, and Rad. Last year’s Keeper was the studio’s most abstract game yet, following a walking lighthouse and a bird as they travel through a surrealist post-apocalyptic environment. It also featured no spoken dialogue, instead using visual indicators to convey emotion and gameplay.

Double Fine’s upcoming project, Kiln, is continuing the studio’s reputation, as it’s a multiplayer brawler involving…pottery? We briefly played 30 minutes of Kiln and spoke with director Derek Brand about how the idea came to be and the game’s long development cycle.

Kiln started as a project under Double Fine’s Amnesia Fortnight 2017 internal game jam, and studio head Tim Schafer ultimately chose for it to go under quiet development. However, it’s been nine years since then, so why did it take so long? The answer is simple: Brand and his team were pulled into other projects while working on Kiln. Early development started on the game in 2019 after he had finished his work on Rad. Then, of course, COVID-19 hits in 2020.

“That was a little weird for a while, and then we really had to finish Psychonauts 2. So my team got pulled in to help finish it,” he explains. “When that was done and wrapped up, then we went back to Kiln. And that's also when we broke off and worked on Keeper.” It wasn’t until after Psychonauts 2 was done that the developers could start ramping up on other projects.

Kiln also originally had a slightly darker atmosphere with goofy-looking spirits, and Brand’s team always wanted a more playful tone. Eventually, they decided on the concept of pottery because of its broad appeal. You can see the remnants of the spirit concept while you’re waiting in Kiln’s hub area. You float around as a small spirit inhabiting big pieces of pottery in preparation for battle. Brand wanted to create a pottery power fantasy and welcome those who wouldn’t normally play games.

“We think there's a lot of potential for people who just like making pots,” Brand says. The team also added different cosmetic attachments to give players a balance on whether they want to decorate their pots. He continues, ”There are some very light-hearted and cute things. Then there's also some heavy metal spikes and more metal materials. So you can really go either way, if you want to.”

Before my hands-on time with Kiln, I took a one-hour pottery session, and I discovered how much force and precision I needed to craft a pot that looked even. You need to push the clay around, splash water on it so it doesn’t dry, and drive your elbows into your hips to stabilize yourself. It’s quite a lot of work! In Kiln, the process to actually create the pot is much easier. You just simply need to use the control sticks to push in the direction you want to mold your creation. Want a taller flask? Just move your control stick upwards.

Early on in development, parts of Kiln felt more like a pottery simulator, but players weren’t jiving with those aspects. “We had some really interesting tech that felt like you had to push the clay around, and it kind of fought you a little bit, and had to get really good over time to make anything interesting out of it,” Brand explains. “And ultimately, it was just really punishing, and people bounced off of that.”

As a result, the team stripped away many of the more complex and difficult sculpting mechanics, and started providing more assists. “We wanted to make sure that if you just held and moved the control stick, that you could make a cool shape,” he says. “So we really tried to find areas where we could reintroduce some of the more realistic aspects of the clay, but do that in a way that was more welcoming and more approachable.”

Depending on how you sculpt your clay and the size of it, your pot can fit into three typical size archetypes: small, medium, and large. They all come with different abilities and attacks, such as swinging a sword or running into enemies. Kiln’s primary multiplayer mode is called “Quench,” where teams of 4vs4 must collect water to put out the kiln fire on the opponent’s side of the field.

While Double Fine defines Kiln as a brawler, I was surprised at how much the matches felt like playing a MOBA. The arena was filled with twists and turns. Navigating them was like defending a lane like in League of Legends. I also wiped out other players while they were trying to transport water, and vice versa. The respawn times allowed players on both sides to regroup and strategize.

Brand notes that the team has actually heard the MOBA comparisons, since the objective of Quench is to attack the enemy base.“We didn't necessarily look at MOBAs, but I think there's a lot of validity in that comparison,” he explains. “Really, we see [Quench] as this is our main game mode right now. We want to expand their game modes, but this is just one game mode that this one feels particularly similar to MOBA objectives.” However, Kiln is still a brawler at heart, and there are plans for future game modes with a larger variety of objectives.

Kiln will be on Xbox Game Pass when it launches on April 23 for PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X|S. Brand wants the game to be a comfortable and accessible experience that you don’t have to sink massive amounts of time into, unlike other online games that demand that you act like it’s a part-time job.

“I see a game that's really great for Game Pass, and we want a bunch of players to play it. It's something you can dive into and not commit a ton of time to. I just want to dive in and make some pottery, fight, and then collect a few cool new things that I can put on my future pots,” he explains. “I think there’s a place for that kind of game.”




https://www.mmorpg.com/interviews/interview-double-fines-kiln-feels-surprisingly-like-a-moba-2000137852
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss – Chapter 1 Guide
item.content:encoded:
Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss is the latest Lovecraftian horror game to hit the market, but beyond its horror elements, it is first and foremost a puzzle game.

Of course, the fun in every puzzle game is solving the puzzle that the developers have put in front of you, but it's also understandable if you need a hint or two. This guide, and the other six on their way, will take you through each chapter of Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss.

For more on Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss, check out my review. You can also check out our PC optimization guide to get the best performance out of your machine.

Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss - Chapter 1 Guide

The first chapter of Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss is designed to introduce you to the gameplay loop the game will run through for the rest of its six chapters, beginning with how you'll be trying to pick up, manipulate, and move through the environment to reach new areas, discover new clues, and use what's in the material world to impact the more occult nature of R'lyeh.

Once you arrive at Mei's place, you'll head left at the door to find a way inside so you can unlock the front door for Elsa. You'll head left from the front door and move a plant out of the way in order to crouch under some debris and find a path inside the house to the second floor.

Once you're inside, head to the first room on your right before going downstairs to find Mei's phone. You'll also find other objects inside Mei's room you can examine on either bedside table. Picking them up and examining them are part of the game's opening tutorials, so take your time to learn the flow of manipulating objects.

After letting Elsa in, you'll be tasked to look for more information about Mei. To your right, in the room where Elsa is digging into Mei's research tablet, you'll find a stone that you can pick up and analyze. This is where the game teaches you how to analyze clues to reveal Sonar frequencies, that will in turn help you discover more clues.

In the next room, you'll find a partially completed image made up of pages stuck to the wall. The pages required to finish the image are on the floor in front of the image and on the desk to your left. The final image is the third image in the gallery below, if you need a reference.

After completing the image and speaking to Elsa, the next part of the chapter kicks off when you head downstairs to the basement. Move towards the kitchen and turn left and you'll find a long black cord to follow that'll lead you downstairs. Once there, walk towards the door behind the stairs and a cutscene will begin.

It's worth noting that there are plenty of things to interact with along the way that'll prompt more dialogue from Noah, Elsa, and Key. There's also voice recordings from Mei to find, and notes of hers to read. You can and should interact with all of these, since the name of the game is always having as much information as possible. But, on the flip side of that is the fact that you don't need to solve every mystery that Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss throws at you. It's important to be able to parse which information is more world building and which information is actively helping you solve the task at hand.

Once you've been into the basement and discovered the organic matter, Elsa and Noah will deduce that it is some sort of portal, and you'll be tasked with finding a way to open it. You'll notice it bears some resemblance to the image you put together below. To open the portal, simply find the correct rocks and place each of them in the proper slots on either side. The second and third images in the gallery below are the correct rocks, for reference.

After you've opened the portal, a cutscene will play, after which you'll take your first steps into R'lyeh. Walk to your left, and another cutscene will eventually kick off and close out the chapter.

There are no upgrades or collectibles to find in this chapter, so taking all the time you want exploring is more about taking the time to learn as much as possible about the different investigation mechanics. There are more coming, and the game front-loads everything in one big shot between the first two chapters, so it's important to get as comfortable with each new mechanic as it comes.

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/cthulhu-the-cosmic-abyss-chapter-1-guide/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Debate-Lord Anime Nippon Sangoku Is 2026’s Must-Watch Thinking Man’s Show
item.content:encoded:
Anime has a storied history with what I like to call “the thinking man’s shows”—cerebral dramas built on double-crosses, galaxy-brained gambits, and verbal sleight-of-hand that reward viewers paying close attention. Its hieroglyphs are carved with greats: Legend of the Galactic Heroes, Code Geass, Death Note, Attack on Titan, The Apothecary Diaries, and, most recently, Orb: On the Movements of the Earth. Just when I resigned myself to 2026 passing without an heir worthy of that pantheon, Nippon Sangoku quietly released, bringing a fiery new animus to the category: debate bro energy. 

Nippon Sangoku: The Three Nations of the Crimson Sun, created by Ikka Matsuki and animated by Studio Kafka, is a political drama set against a backdrop of a collapsing Japan. According to its long-winded opening narration—which, mercifully, reappears every episode to handhold viewers through the minutiae of its political hellscape—Japan is in a very “those who don’t learn from history are doomed to repeat it” point in its life. Everything that can go wrong does: political corruption, acts of God, and nuclear warfare between the United States, Russia, the United Kingdom, Germany, and India thrash Japan’s economy so thoroughly that the Reiwa era (contemporary Japan, circa 2019) reverts back to something resembling the Meiji Era (1868 to 1912, when Japan started flirting with interacting with the Western world). 

In the aftermath, Japan fractures into three warring nations, each clawing for dominance through military might and puppy guarding resources. It’s basically what Alex Garland’s A24 movie Civil War was reaching for tonally if the film hadn’t been trying to be white folks’ City of God by way of HBO’s The Newsroom (and AI slop posters). 

At the center of its tale is Aoteru Misumi, a man the show delights in roasting as the most milquetoast of protagonists. He is a meek man with a basic bitch side-part that’d leave him lost in a crowd of extras, and he doesn't even have a nepo-baby pedigree to stand on. If anything, the most likable thing about him is his wife, Saki. Aoteru is also miles away from the kind of person you’d see at the front lines of Japan’s woke 2 like his street-smart wife, who also can’t help but voice her polite disappointment at how he keeps his gifts tucked away, treating them as passive talents rather than tools to get active with instilling real change in Japan. 

And Saki’s right, because what Aoteru does have is vital to her dreams of a restored Japan. Beneath all his self-effacing caution is a librarian with an historian’s grasp of humanity’s cycles of folly, ego, delusion, and breakthroughs. It’s also a mind, tragically, that’s smarter than he ever lets the world see, satisfied with living peacefully under the heel of Japan on the verge of a dictatorship than rock the boat with ideas of unification attained without force. 

Translation: Aoteru is your everyday moderate liberal man. 

That is, until tragedy strikes, a little too close to home, and he finally gets active as a legendary military strategist who aims to unify Japan on the strength of his sharp insight and persuasive gift of gab. 

Admittedly, an anime about a man going full Hasan Piker, verbally dismantling his opposition’s interlocutors, sounds wonderfully boring on paper in the big 2026. We’re living in un-parody-able times, after all. Yet, even in its early episodes, Nippon Sangoku has already become a standout for me. A huge part of that is because the show feels spiritually like Golden Kamuy, with an ensemble of larger-than-life weirdos whose unserious hijinks make you forget that Nippon Sangoku is, at its core, a political drama. And at the heart of that drama is the ideological clash between Aoteru and his sardonic, Sasuke-esque rival, Yoshitsune, whose “might is right” approach to unification is the polar opposite of Aoteru’s rhetoric-driven idealism. 

Another huge reason I’m enamored with Nippon Sangoku is how beautifully it’s animated. Without fail, the show turns what could’ve been a monotonous string of “debate‑bro destroys his ops” moments that’d win the internet on LiveStreamFails into something genuinely fascinating. And even as someone with only a college‑level grasp of Shōwa‑era history, I never felt lost in the political minutiae, proper‑noun avalanches, or policy deep‑cuts Aoteru fires off.

That’s thanks to the show’s clarity and expressive visual storytelling, which uses clever cues, asides, and metaphors to translate its double‑entendre political speak into something legible and lively. It eases viewers into its heady themes so gracefully that what could’ve played like a PBS history special disguised as anime instead becomes one of the most enthralling watches in an already packed season. It's the most fun I’ve had with an anime, much less a show, since Hulu’s Shōgun. 

Kafka really did their big one here. Given the production house’s work on The Ancient Magus’ Bride, one of Tatsuki Fujimoto’s stronger 17-26 one-shot anthology stories, and the third Mononoke film (not Studio Ghibli), Nippon Sangoku feels like a bravura showcase of the studio’s strengths. In one moment, the anime is a stirring epic with the fate of an entire nation at stake; in the next, it’s the most catch-you-off-guard funny misadventure committed to animation. All the while, it’s punctuated by an “oh shit” cliffhanger that’ll have your brain buzzing as the full tapestry of its dense world continues to take shape. 

The only downside is that Nippon Sangoku is a Prime Video exclusive show—a streaming platform where anime go to die in the algorithmic void without a peep of social media promotion. But with any luck, that obscurity, paired with it being one of the rare anime Hideo Kojima has spotlighted twice on his mostly Letterboxd-coded social media profiles, gives Nippon Sangoku the air of a hidden gem. And in its early goings, it absolutely earns that status.

Here’s hoping the show continues sharpening itself into a true thinking-man’s anime with teeth that gnash deeper with every new episode. 



https://aftermath.site/nippon-sangoku-anime-prime-video/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Ball X Pit The Shadow Update will release on April 27th
item.content:encoded:
Kenny Sun has revealed that the next free update for Ball X Pit will be launching on April 27th, and that update is the Shadow Update. That means players will get two new characters, 11 new balls, a brand new building, and four new passives. The full details have been given by Kenny Sun, and you can see the Ball X Pit The Shadow Update content below the teaser.

Ball X Pit The Shadow Update content details

  • The Tunneller is a scrappy miner whose shots don’t stop at the edge; they wrap around the screen and come back in from the other side, making just about every angle fair game.
  • The Tiptoer is a fragile but deadly ninja who stays undetected by enemies at close range at the cost of lower health and damage.
  • Venom stacks up with each hit, slowing targets and chipping away at them until enough buildup finally locks them in place with paralysis.
  • Time leans into area control, dropping a timesnare that freezes enemies caught in its range.
  • Erosion cuts straight through foes, with each hit shaving off a percentage of their current health, making it incredibly nasty against tougher targets.
  • Time Bomb adds a bit of delayed chaos, planting an explosive that goes off after a short wait.
  • Timestop does exactly what it sounds like, freezing absolutely everything on the field for a few seconds before going on cooldown.
  • Warp keeps things unpredictable, blinking to a random spot on the field upon every enemy hit while ramping up speed as it goes.
  • Sniper shots cut straight through enemies but burn out the moment they hit a wall or loop back, trading longevity for a boosted critical chance that rewards precise enemy line ups.
  • Heart Swallower gives each hit a certain chance to drain a bit of life and heal you for one while also weakening the target’s attack by a percentage, but remember each enemy can only be drained once.
  • Full Metal Rapier thrives on chaos with each ball scaling its damage based on how many baby balls and enemies are currently on the field.
  • Arrow of Fate flips defense into offense, making you immune to incoming projectiles and firing back a burst of baby balls whenever you’re hit.
  • Argent Stopwatch plays with momentum, starting balls off at 150% damage before shaving off 30% with every bounce, so timing and positioning matter more than ever.
  • Sword Breaker keeps things steady but scalable, lowering base damage to 75% while quietly stacking an extra 1% for every enemy in play.

  • Better start thinking of a spot to drop the Guildhall! This new building will allow players to re-roll previously chosen character upgrades.

Ball X Pit Review – Sphere factor

In our Ball X Pit review, Dom said, “Ball X Pit carries the spirit of an arcade classic, while imbuing it with modern roguelike ideas. It’s immediately one of the best indie games of the year.”

Source: Steam



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/20/ball-x-pit-the-shadow-update-will-release-on-april-27th/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Special Ruleset Vengeance Campaign is Back Today in The Elder Scrolls Online
item.content:encoded:
Things have been pretty busy in and around The Elder Scrolls Online in recent weeks. for the PvPers, the ESO team has got you, with a new Vengeance campaign starting today, with some important changes.

The Vengeance campaign is a special ruleset PvP mode in Cyrodiil that levels the playing field by limiting everyone eligible (levels 10 to 50) to build templates and normalized stats. When you enter, your character will change to match a certain build template for their class, and you'll get a backpack of supplies. The only things you take with you when the campaign ends are XP, gold, Alliance Points, and Champion Points. (The Champion System is disabled during the campaign.)

This time around, the campaign will run through April 27th and while this campaign is smaller in scope, there are a few changes to be aware of. First, some changes are intended to increase power for DPS players. Now, you can target more enemies using area of effect abilities, going up to six from a cap of three. These tweaks should speed things up and make them more aggressive. It should also impact healing, with more enemies targeted at once, you’lve got to be strategic and active if you’re the one doing the healing.

You can take part in this latest round via the Alliance War menu. More details on this month's campaign and Vengeance overall is over here in the announcement. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/the-special-ruleset-vengeance-campaign-is-back-today-in-the-elder-scrolls-online-2000137853
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
